aedes_mosquito.habitat: Stagnant water, urban areas, flower pots, tires
aedes_mosquito.ecological_role: Part of food chain for birds and bats
precautions: Use mosquito repellent (DEET-based)
Eliminate stagnant water around home
Use mosquito nets while sleeping
Wear full-sleeve clothing at dawn/dusk
Install window and door screens
aedes_mosquito.immediate_action: If bitten and fever develops within 3-14 days, see a doctor immediately
aedes_mosquito.fun_fact: Only female mosquitoes bite - they need blood protein to develop eggs

honeybee.allergy_risk: High - can trigger anaphylactic shock in sensitive individuals
honeybee.bioindicator: Healthy bee population means healthy flowering plant ecosystem
honeybee.ecological_role: Critical pollinator - responsible for 1/3 of global food supply
precautions: Do not swat at bees - stay calm and move away slowly
Avoid wearing strong perfumes near flowering areas
Carry EpiPen if you have known bee allergy
Do not disturb hives
Wear light-colored clothing outdoors
honeybee.immediate_action: Remove stinger by scraping (not pinching). Apply ice. Seek emergency care if allergic reaction occurs.
honeybee.fun_fact: A honeybee visits 50-100 flowers during a single collection trip

house_spider.habitat: Corners of rooms, basements, garages, window frames
house_spider.bioindicator: Presence indicates other insects are present indoors
house_spider.ecological_role: Natural pest controller - eats mosquitoes, flies, and other insects
precautions: Seal cracks and gaps in walls and windows
Reduce clutter where spiders hide
Use a glass and paper to relocate - do not kill
Vacuum webs regularly
house_spider.immediate_action: Generally harmless. If bitten, clean with antiseptic. Monitor for unusual swelling.
house_spider.fun_fact: House spiders can go months without food and help control household pest populations

german_cockroach.habitat: Kitchens, bathrooms, drains, behind appliances, food storage areas
german_cockroach.allergy_risk: High - cockroach allergens are a major asthma trigger
german_cockroach.bioindicator: Presence indicates poor sanitation or moisture issues
precautions: Store food in sealed containers
Fix leaky pipes and eliminate moisture
Seal entry points like pipes and gaps in walls
Clean kitchen surfaces and appliances regularly
Use boric acid or cockroach bait traps
german_cockroach.immediate_action: Do not consume food that cockroaches may have contacted. Clean and disinfect affected areas immediately.
german_cockroach.fun_fact: German cockroaches can develop resistance to pesticides within a few generations

dragonfly.habitat: Near clean ponds, lakes, rivers, wetlands
dragonfly.bioindicator: Presence means clean unpolluted water is nearby. Dragonfly larvae require high water quality.
dragonfly.ecological_role: Apex insect predator - controls mosquito and fly populations naturally
precautions: No precautions needed - completely harmless to humans
Protect their habitat by keeping water bodies clean
dragonfly.immediate_action: No action needed. Dragonflies are completely harmless!
dragonfly.fun_fact: Dragonflies have a 95% hunting success rate - the highest of any predator on Earth
